subject: ROBOT e2e test cases improvements. Add DCAE tls init container to robot pod for cert secure communication with DCAE application
commit_message:
ROBOT e2e test cases improvements. Add DCAE tls init container to robot pod for cert secure communication with DCAE application

Issue-ID: INT-1588

Signed-off-by: Krzysztof Kuzmicki <krzysztof.kuzmicki@nokia.com>
Change-Id: I757420b6e3a76a046e6478f6e46d9a672d459bd1

entries.message:
Patch Set 1:

Gitlab controller: Deployment put on queue, number 7 on the queue.

Patch Set 1:

Hi

I would like to add additional test cases that would communicate with VES and HVVES using aaf certificates instead basic auth sample1/sample1. Also using this init we would remove exec script for HVVES SSL test case.
Using:
root@dev-robot-5b9dc4886f-sz78p:/# ll /share/certs/dcae-init/
total 60
drwxrwxrwx 3 root root 4096 May 11 16:08 ./
drwxr-xr-x 3 root root 4096 May 11 16:08 ../
-rw-r--r-- 1 root root 1907 May 11 16:08 cacert.pem
-rw-r--r-- 1 root root 5299 May 11 16:08 cert.pem
-rw-r--r-- 1 root root 1859 May 11 16:08 key.pem

drwxr-xr-x 2 root root 4096 May 11 16:08 local/
-rw-r--r-- 1 root root 24 May 11 16:08 p12.pass
-rw-r--r-- 1 root root 1413 May 11 16:08 trust.jks
-rw-r--r-- 1 root root 24 May 11 16:08 trust.pass
-rw-r--r-- 1 root root 4886 May 11 16:08 cert.jks
-rw-r--r-- 1 root root 5399 May 11 16:08 cert.p12
-rw-r--r-- 1 root root 24 May 11 16:08 jks.pass

We can establish secure communication with DCAE applictaons.

Patch Set 1:

(4 comments)

We might consider some little naming changes here.

Patch Set 2:

Gitlab controller: Deployment put on queue, number 7 on the queue.

Patch Set 2: Code-Review-1

In my humble opinion it's a terrible idea.

As far as I understand (let me know if I'm wrong) this would make robot depend on AAF and we don't want that.

Robot is not considered to be integral part of ONAP. At some point we may want to deploy robot totally outside of k8s cluster in which ONAP is running to make the tests more from the end user perspective. In my opinion if you need a certificate you should retrieve it as a part of your testcase and clearly fail the test if AAF is not responding but don't try to integrate robot pod with AAF

Patch Set 2: Code-Review-1

good points by krzysztof, didnt realize what this pod was doing.

Patch Set 2:

Gitlab Orange 4: Chained-CI build started, max time: 14400 seconds
